results.title: Delhi govt clears ₹400-crore start-up policy, to set up incubation centres
results.description: New Delhi: The Delhi Cabinet has approved the Delhi Start-up and Incubation policy, a ₹400-crore plan to establish incubation centres across state-run higher education institutions and provide financial support to start-ups over the next five years, according to an official statement released on Thursday

results.title: Gambhir-led Indian management breaks silence on Rohit Sharma amidst retirement rumours says, 'No pressure...'
results.description: The Indian team management has firmly dismissed assertions that former captain Rohit Sharma is under intense scrutiny from the team hierarchy.

results.title: Gangster Nitish Kaushal arrested in U.S.’s Vermont: FBI
results.description: FBI arrests gangster Nitish Kaushal in Vermont, targeting the Bhagwanpuria gang amid Operation Hard Ball enforcement actions.

results.title: Twenty-nine countries sign agreement to establish global AI cooperation body
results.description: Twenty-nine countries on Thursday signed an agreement to establish the World AI Cooperation Organization, an intergovernmental body China says aims to promote international ‌cooperation ⁠and ⁠global governance in artificial intelligence. Representatives ​from the 29 countries, including Russia, Belarus, ​Serbia, Cuba, Brazil and Venezuela, as well as 10 African and ​12 Asian countries, ⁠signed the ‌agreement as founding members. The organization’s headquarters will be ⁠located in Shanghai, Chinese state agency Xinhua said.

results.title: Teja Singh Samundri is a hero for Sikhism & India
results.description: Sardar Teja Singh Samundri’s legacy endures in the values he embodied, including service without self-interest, courage without hatred and faith without fear.

results.title: Delhi HC gives Kejriwal, others one last chance to reply in excise policy case
results.description: The Delhi High Court on Thursday granted a final opportunity to former chief minister Arvind Kejriwal, former deputy chief minister Manish Sisodia and AAP leader Durgesh Pathak to file their replies to the Central Bureau of Investigation’s (CBI) appeal challenging a trial court order discharging them in the excise policy case
